name: Fresh and Salt Cod Cakes/Jalapeno Tartar/6
recipeInstructions:
Notes: On Cod, soak in water overnight, changing water several times, rinse and drain. The cod is soaked if the mix is slightly salty and the fish has plumped up to nearly double its original size. Soaking cod too long results in fish with no taste.
In a large saucepan, mix together the soaked and drained salt cod, water, and milk. Tie bay leaf, thyme, and parsley in a 4-inch square piece of cheesecloth and place it in the cod, water, and milk mixture. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low and cook for 15 minutes. Add fresh cod and cook for 5 more minutes. Drain and remove fish to a large bowl to cool, discarding flavoring ingredients. Flake fish with a fork and set aside.
In a large skillet, melt butter until sizzling, add celery and chopped onion and cook until onion is translucent (about 5 minutes). Add cooked vegetables to flaked fish, egg, and mix. Then add green onions, chives, parsley, cayenne pepper, tabasco sauce, dry mustard, Worcestershire Sauce, mayonnaise, lemon juice, bread crumbs, and salt and pepper to taste.
Form mixture into 14 to 16 patties (each patty is about 3-4 inches). Place on a baking sheet and cover. Refrigerate for 1 hour.
If the mixture is too wet, add more breadcrumbs as needed. When ready to cook, whisk egg yolks with milk in a small bowl. Dip codcakes into flour mixture, then egg and milk mixture, and finally dredge in remaining breadcrumbs. Repeat with remainder of codcakes.
In a large skillet, melt butter and oil together. Fry half the codcakes about 3-4 minutes per side until golden brown. Drain fishcakes on paper towels. Repeat with remaining cakes.
Serve warm with Jalapeno Tartar Sauce and fresh lemon or lime wedges.
